steps.title: Identify the condition and restricted sample space
steps.content: At least 10 years of age means participants aged 10–19 or 20+. Looking at the table totals for these age ranges: 10–19 has 30 participants, 20+ has 30 participants. Total restricted sample: 60 participants.

steps.title: Count Group A within the restricted sample
steps.content: From the table, Group A has 11 participants aged 10–19 and 4 participants aged 20+. Total Group A participants at least 10 years old: $11 + 4 = 15$.

steps.title: Apply conditional probability formula
steps.content:
$$P(A \mid \text{at least 10}) = \frac{15}{60} = \frac{1}{4}$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
